title: lathyrus sylvestris var. heterophyllus
intro: Lathyrus sylvestris var. heterophyllus, also known as the wild pea or vetch pea, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its unique, heterophyllous leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant's seeds have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The seeds have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to their rich content of isoleucine, a branched-chain amino acid with potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the seeds are typically dried and ground into a powder or made into a decoction, which can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or capsules.
